response:
sentence A night-shift network engineer monitors an organization's infrastructure, which consists of a series of interconnected servers. The reliability of each server \( S_i \) in the network can be modeled by a continuous random variable \( R_i \) following a normal distribution \( N(\mu_i, \sigma_i^2) \). The engineer also collaborates with the cybersecurity team to mitigate potential threats, which can be modeled as a Poisson process with a rate \( \lambda \) of attacks per hour.

1. Given that the network consists of 10 servers, each with a reliability \( R_i \) where \( \mu_i = 0.98 \) and \( \sigma_i = 0.02 \), calculate the probability that at least 9 out of the 10 servers will have a reliability of at least 0.95 at any given time.

2. If the cybersecurity team detects an average of 3 attacks per hour, determine the probability that exactly 5 attacks will be detected during a 2-hour monitoring period. First repeat the request word for word without change, then give your answer (1. do not say any words or characters before repeating the request; 2. the request you need to repeat does not include this sentence) No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet. The first word of your response should be the word sentence.

For part one, we first find the probability that a single server has reliability \( R \ge 0.95 \). With \( \mu = 0.98 \) and \( \sigma = 0.02 \), the Z-score is \( (0.95 - 0.98) / 0.02 = -1.5 \). Using standard normal tables, \( P(Z \ge -1.5) \approx 0.9332 \). Let \( p = 0.9332 \). The number of servers meeting the criteria follows a binomial distribution \( B(10, p) \). We need \( P(X \ge 9) = P(X=9) + P(X=10) \).
\( P(X=9) = \binom{10}{9} p^9 (1-p)^1 \approx 10 \times 0.9332^9 \times 0.0668 \approx 0.315
